title: Standard-S: PCR barcoding of SARS-CoV-2 S gen amplicons for Nanopore sequencing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8niki","text":"Most of the defining mutations of the severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus-2 (SARS-CoV-2) variants of concern (VOCs) have been identified in the S gene sequence. For this reason, S-based lineage assignment is possible using the current nomenclature system. We have developed a protocol for overlapping amplification of the S gene sequence using previously reported primer sequences (V3 primers of ARTIC Network) in combination with a PCR barcoding approach of the samples for Nanopore sequencing plataforms. This protocol allows the screening of multiple COVID-19 positive samples for lineage/clade assignment and mutational surveillance of the spike gene. Additionally, this protocol can be is easily adapted for dual barcoding using the \"Ligation sequencing amplicons - dual barcoding (SQK-LSK109 with EXP-NBD104, EXP-NBD114, and EXP-PBC096)\" Nanopore protocol to upscale the number of samples per run.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1ro0t","text":"","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1ep8v","text":"","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Keeping the SARS-CoV-2 extracted RNA samples on ice all the time and spin down the tubes.

Set up the first round PCR reaction in a pre-PCR cabinet for primer pool A and primer pool B Reagent PCR master mix pool A PCR master mix pool B 1 Nuclease free water 400 μL 400 μL 2 Primer pool A (30 μM) 25 μL -- 3 Primer pool B (30 μM) 25 μL 4 Q5® Hot Start High-Fidelity 2X Master Mix 625 μL 625 μL 5 Final volume 1050 μL 1050 μL Table 2: S gene PCR amplification master mixes pool A an B. Keep the reversed transcribed sample on a PCR cooler or ice.
Spin down the tubes and pool all samples in a 1.5 mL LoBind tube. If barcode balance is desired, barcoded samples should be individually purified using AMPure XP beads 0.5X and then equimolar pooled.

Set up the second round PCR reaction in a pre-PCR cabinet for primer pool A and primer pool B as follows: Reagent Volume (µL) 1 Q5® Hot Start High-Fidelity 2X Master Mix 6.00 2 PCR Barcodes (BC01-BC96) 1.00 3 Final volume 6.75 Table 4: S gene PCR barcoding mix using the Q5® Hot Start High-Fidelity polymerase.

Prepare the following adapter ligation mix: Reagent Volume (uL) 1 Clean end-prepped DNA 60 2 Ligation Buffer (LNB) 25 3 NEBNext Quick T4 DNA Ligase 10 4 AMX adapter 5 5 Final volume 100 Table 8: Adapter (AMX) ligation mix.
Use the Nanopore standard procedure for priming the FLO-MIN106D or FLO-FLG001 flow cells. https://community.nanoporetech.com/nanopore_learning/lessons/priming-and-loading-your-flow-cell

Use epi2me-labs/wf-artic V1 scheme for consensus generation wf-artic hedgehog for lineage set assignment using maximum ambiguity hedgehog Use president for S gene completeness president and samtools for average sequencing depth samtools

Set up the RT-PCR reaction tubes in a clean pre-PCR cabinet by adding 2 μL of LunaScript® RT SuperMix to each PCR tube. Include a RT-PCR negative control by replacing RNA sample with nuclease free water. Add positive and negative controls if available for results validation.
Add 8 uL of sample to the tube containing the LunaScript® RT SuperMix and mix gently. The final volume of the reaction is 10 μL.

Incubate in a thermal cycler using the the following instructions: Step Temperature (ºC) Time Cycles 1 Primer annealing 25 2 min 1 2 cDNA Synthesis 55 10 min 3 Heat inactivation 95 1 min 4 Hold 10 - Table 1: SARS-CoV-2 RT-PCR thermal profile.
Transfer 10,5 μL of the PCR Master mix Pool A to the PCR tube set A and 10,5 μL of the PCR Master mix Pool B to the PCR tube set B, respectively.

Using a multichannel pipette, transfer 2 μL of reversed transcribed product from the Reverse Transcription step to the corresponding Pool A and Pool B PCR tube. Carry over the negative and positive controls. Store at -20 ºC the remaining reverse transcribed sample for further characterization, if needed.

Spin down the PCR tubes and incubate in the thermal cycler with the following program: Step Temperature (ºC) Time Cycles 1 Initial denaturation 98 30 sec 1 2 Denaturation 98 15 sec 20 3 Annealing and extension 63 3 min 4 Hold 10 -- -- Table 3: S gene PCR amplification using the Q5® Hot Start High-Fidelity polymerase.

Remove the tubes from the thermal cycler and spin down briefly. Mix the volume of pool A and pool B in the PCR tube set B. Up to this point it is highly recommended to check pool A and pool B amplification of the positive and negative controls and some random samples in a 1% agarose gel electrophoresis. Amplicons are expected to have ~1 Kb.
Prepare a set of PCR tubes with 9 μL of nuclease free water for a 1:10 dilution of the first round PCR amplification.

Using a multichannel pipette, add 5 µL of the dilution 1:10 of the first round amplification of each sample. Spin down and incubate in the thermal cycler as follows: Step Temperature (ºC) Time Cycles 1 Initial denaturation 98 30 s 1 2 Denaturation 98 15 s 15 3 Annealing 62 15 s 4 Extension 72 1m 5 Final extension 72 10 m 1 6 Hold 4 -- -- Table 5: PCR barcoding thermal cycling profile

Add 0.5X volume of Ampure XP beads. Incubate 5 minutes in a rotator mixer. Spin down and rest the tubes in a magnetic rack for PCR tubes for 5 minutes. Make sure AMPure XP beads reached room temperature before use.
Discard the supernatant by aspiration, taking care not to disturb the pellet beads.
Wash the beads with Ethanol 70%. Repeat this step.

Let the pellet air dry for ~30 seconds and add 50 uL of nuclease free water. Incubate for 2 minutes at room temperature, spin down and place the PCR tubes in the magnetic rack for 5 minutes. Recover the supernatant.
Quantify the clean barcoded pool using a suitable fluorometric assay.

Prepare the end prep mix as follows: Sample/Reagent Volume (µL) 1 Clean barcoded pool (1 µg) 48 2 NEBNext FFPE DNA Repair Buffer 3.5 3 NEBNext FFPE DNA Repair Mix 2 4 Ultra II End-prep reaction buffer 3.5 5 Ultra II End-prep enzyme mix 3 6 Final volume 60 Table 6: End prep reaction mix.
Prepare 1 µg of clean barcoded pool in 48 µL. Take the barcoded pool the repair and end-prep step or store the DNA at 4 ºC overnight or -20 ºC for long term storage.

Incubate the End-prep mix in the thermal cycler as follows: Step Temperature (ºC) Time 1 Enzymes incubation 20 5 min 2 Enzymes inactivation 65 5 min 3 Hold 4 -- Table 7: End prep and DNA repair thermal incubation
(Optional) Add 1X volume of AMPure XP beads to the reaction and incubate in the rotator mixer for 5 minutes. Spin down and rest the tubes in the magnetic rack for 5 minutes.
Discard the supernatant by aspiration, taking care not to disturb the pellet beads.
Wash the pellet with Ethanol 70% twice.
Let the pellet air dry for ~30 seconds and add 61 µLof nuclease free water and incubate at room temperature for 2 minutes.
Spin down and rest the tubes in the magnetic rack for 5 minutes and recover the supernatant. Take forward the end-prepped DNA into the adapter ligation step or store the sample at 4 ºC overnight
Incubate the reaction for 10 minutes at room temperature. Do not incubate the reaction for longer than 10 minutes if the AMPure XP beads purification was omitted.
Spin down the tubes and add AMPure XP beads 0.4X volume. Incubate in the rotator mixer for 5 minutes at room temperature.
Spin down the tubes and rest the tubes in the magnet rack for 5 minutes. Pipette off the supernatant.
Add 200 µL of Short Fragment Buffer (SFB) and mix gently. Spin down and place the tube in the magnet rack for 5 minutes. Discard the supernatant.
Repeat the previous step.
Spin down the tube and place it in the magnet rack. Remove any residual SFB and let it air dry for ~30 seconds.

Remove the tubes from the magnet and add 15 µL of Elution Buffer (EB). Flick the tube, spin down briefly and incubate the tube for 10 minutes at 37 ºC. The final library can be stored for up to 3 days at 4 ºC.
Quantify the final library using a fluorometric assay.

Once the flow cell is correclty primer proceed to load the library mixing the following: Sample/Reagent Volume (uL) 1 Sequencing Buffer (SQB) 37.5 2 Loading Beads (LB) 25.5 3 DNA library (~300 ng) 12 4 Final volume 75 Table 9: DNA library to load in a FLO-MIN106D. Sample/Reagent Volume (uL) 1 Sequencing Buffer (SQB) 15 2 Loading Beads (LB) 10 3 DNA library (~200 ng) 5 4 Final Volume 30 Table 9: DNA library to load in a FLO-FLG001. We recommend to load initially 300 ng for the FLO-MIN106D and 200 ng for the FLO-FLG001 and monitor the pore occupancy over the first 20 minutes and reload if the pore occupancy is lower than 60%. Additionally, a refuel after 12 hours of the sequencing run is recommended. Note that the amount of DNA library is significantly higher than the amount recommended by Oxford Nanopore (20-50 fmol). If not sure, begin from this number and upscale accordingly to reach a satisfactory pore occupancy.
creator: Cecilia Salazar
